Clarification of Existing Standards, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–59, title VI, § 6009(b)119 Stat. 1876Aug. 10, 2005“(1).—In generalNot later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of Transportation] shall (in consultation with affected agencies and interested parties) promulgate regulations that clarify the factors to be considered and the standards to be applied in determining the prudence and feasibility of alternatives under and , United States Code.Aug. 10, 2005section 138 of title 23section 303 of title 49“(2).—RequirementsThe regulations—“(A)shall clarify the application of the legal standards to a variety of different types of transportation programs and projects depending on the circumstances of each case; and“(B)may include, as appropriate, examples to facilitate clear and consistent interpretation by agency decisionmakers.”

Study of Transit Needs in National Parks and Related Public Lands, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–178, title III, § 3039112 Stat. 393Pub. L. 105–206, title IX, § 9009(y)112 Stat. 862June 9, 1998July 22, 1998“(a).—PurposesThe purposes of this section are to encourage and promote the development of transportation systems for the betterment of the national parks and other units of the National Park System, national wildlife refuges, recreational areas, and other public lands in order to conserve natural, historical, and cultural resources and prevent adverse impact, relieve congestion, minimize transportation fuel consumption, reduce pollution (including noise and visual pollution), and enhance visitor mobility and accessibility and the visitor experience.“(b)Study.—“(1).—In generalThe Secretary, in coordination with the Secretary of the Interior, shall undertake a comprehensive study of alternative transportation needs in national parks and related public lands managed by Federal land management agencies [to] assist in carrying out the purposes described in subsection (a). The study shall be submitted to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate not later than .January 1, 2000“(2).—Study elementsThe study required by paragraph (1) shall—“(A)identify transportation strategies that improve the management of the national parks and related public lands;“(B)identify national parks and related public lands with existing and potential problems of adverse impact, high congestion, and pollution, or which can benefit from alternative transportation modes;“(C)assess the feasibility of alternative transportation modes; and“(D)identify and estimate the costs of alternative transportation modes for each of the national parks and related public lands referred to in paragraph (1).“(3).—DefinitionFor purposes of this subsection, the term ‘Federal land management agencies’ means the National Park Service, the United States Fish and Wildlife Service, and the Bureau of Land Management.”

Study of Alternative Transportation Modes in National Park System, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–240, title I, § 1050105 Stat. 2000Dec. 18, 1991“(a).—In GeneralNot later than 12 months after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior, shall conduct and transmit to Congress a study of alternative transportation modes for use in the National Park System. In conducting such study, the Secretary shall consider (1) the economic and technical feasibility, environmental effects, projected costs and benefits as compared to the costs and benefits of existing transportation systems, and general suitability of transportation modes that would provide efficient and environmentally sound ingress to and egress from National Park lands; and (2) methods to obtain private capital for the construction of such transportation modes and related infrastructure.Dec. 18, 1991“(b).—FundingFrom sums authorized to be appropriated for park roads and parkways for fiscal year 1992, $300,000 shall be available to carry out this section.”

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: It is the national policy that special effort should be made to preserve the natural beauty of the countryside and public park and recreation lands, wildlife and waterfowl refuges, and historic sites.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall cooperate and consult with the Secretaries of the Interior, Housing and Urban Development, and Agriculture, and with the States in developing transportation plans and programs that include measures to maintain or enhance the natural beauty of the lands traversed.

children.label: I
children.text: provide an evaluation under this section to the Secretaries described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: II
children.text: provide a period of 30 days for receipt of comments.

children.heading: .—Assumed acceptance
children.label: ii .—Assumed acceptance
children.text: If the Secretary does not receive comments by 15 days after the deadline under clause (i)(II), the Secretary shall assume a lack of objection and proceed with the action.

children.label: I
children.text: subsections (b) through (f); or

children.label: II
children.text: the consultation process under ; orsection 306108 of title 54

children.label: ii
children.text: programmatic section 4(f) evaluations, as described in regulations issued by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: there is no feasible and prudent alternative to the use of the land; and

children.label: B
children.text: the program includes all possible planning to minimize harm to such park, recreational area, wildlife and waterfowl refuge, or historic site resulting from such use.

children.heading: .—Studies
children.label: 4 .—Studies
children.text: In carrying out the national policy declared in this section the Secretary, in cooperation with the Secretary of the Interior and appropriate State and local officials, is authorized to conduct studies as to the most feasible Federal-aid routes for the movement of motor vehicular traffic through or around national parks so as to best serve the needs of the traveling public while preserving the natural beauty of these areas.

children.heading: .—Requirements for historic sites
children.label: A .—Requirements for historic sites
children.text: The requirements of this section shall be considered to be satisfied with respect to an area described in paragraph (2) if the Secretary determines, in accordance with this subsection, that a transportation program or project will have a de minimis impact on the area.

children.heading: .—Requirements for parks, recreation areas, and wildlife or waterfowl refuges
children.label: B .—Requirements for parks, recreation areas, and wildlife or waterfowl refuges
children.text: The requirements of subsection (a)(1) shall be considered to be satisfied with respect to an area described in paragraph (3) if the Secretary determines, in accordance with this subsection, that a transportation program or project will have a de minimis impact on the area. The requirements of subsection (a)(2) with respect to an area described in paragraph (3) shall not include an alternatives analysis.

children.heading: .—Criteria
children.label: C .—Criteria
children.text: In making any determination under this subsection, the Secretary shall consider to be part of a transportation program or project any avoidance, minimization, mitigation, or enhancement measures that are required to be implemented as a condition of approval of the transportation program or project.

children.label: i
children.text: the transportation program or project will have no adverse effect on the historic site; or

children.label: ii
children.text: there will be no historic properties affected by the transportation program or project;

children.label: B
children.text: the finding of the Secretary has received written concurrence from the applicable State historic preservation officer or tribal historic preservation officer (and from the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation if the Council is participating in the consultation process); and

children.label: C
children.text: the finding of the Secretary has been developed in consultation with parties consulting as part of the process referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Parks, recreation areas, and wildlife or waterfowl refuges
children.label: 3 .—Parks, recreation areas, and wildlife or waterfowl refuges
A
children.text: the Secretary has determined, after public notice and opportunity for public review and comment, that the transportation program or project will not adversely affect the activities, features, and attributes of the park, recreation area, or wildlife or waterfowl refuge eligible for protection under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the finding of the Secretary has received concurrence from the officials with jurisdiction over the park, recreation area, or wildlife or waterfowl refuge.

children.heading: Satisfaction of Requirements for Certain Historic Sites.—
children.label: c Satisfaction of Requirements for Certain Historic Sites.—
A
children.text: align, to the maximum extent practicable, with the requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) and , including implementing regulations; and42 U.S.C. 4321section 306108 of title 54

children.label: B
children.text: not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this subsection, coordinate with the Secretary of the Interior and the Executive Director of the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation (referred to in this subsection as the “Council”) to establish procedures to satisfy the requirements described in subparagraph (A) (including regulations).

children.label: i
children.text: include the determination of the Secretary in the analysis required under that Act;

children.label: I
children.text: each applicable State historic preservation officer and tribal historic preservation officer;

children.label: II
children.text: the Council, if the Council is participating in the consultation process under ; andsection 306108 of title 54

children.label: III
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: iii
children.text: request from the applicable preservation officer, the Council, and the Secretary of the Interior a concurrence that the determination is sufficient to satisfy subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: .—Concurrence
children.label: B .—Concurrence
children.text: If the applicable preservation officer, the Council, and the Secretary of the Interior each provide a concurrence requested under subparagraph (A)(iii), no further analysis under subsection (a)(1) shall be required.

children.label: i
children.text: be included in the record of decision or finding of no significant impact of the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be posted on an appropriate Federal website by not later than 3 days after the date of receipt by the Secretary of all concurrences requested under subparagraph (A)(iii).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: If the Secretary, the applicable preservation officer, the Council, and the Secretary of the Interior concur that no feasible and prudent alternative exists as described in paragraph (2), the Secretary may provide to the applicable preservation officer, the Council, and the Secretary of the Interior notice of the intent of the Secretary to satisfy subsection (a)(2) through the consultation requirements of .section 306108 of title 54

children.heading: .—Satisfaction of conditions
children.label: B .—Satisfaction of conditions
children.text: To satisfy subsection (a)(2), each individual described in paragraph (2)(A)(ii) shall concur in the treatment of the applicable historic site described in the memorandum of agreement or programmatic agreement developed under .section 306108 of title 54

children.heading: References to Past Transportation Environmental Authorities.—
children.label: d References to Past Transportation Environmental Authorities.—

children.heading: .—Section 4(f) requirements
children.label: 1 .—Section 4(f) requirements
children.text: The requirements of this section are commonly referred to as section 4(f) requirements (see section 4(f) of the Department of Transportation Act (; ) as in effect before the repeal of that section).Public Law 89–67080 Stat. 934

children.heading: .—Section 106 requirements
children.label: 2 .—Section 106 requirements
children.text: The requirements of are commonly referred to as section 106 requirements (see section 106 of the National Historic Preservation Act of 1966 (; ) as in effect before the repeal of that section).section 306108 of title 54Public Law 89–66580 Stat. 917

children.heading: .—Bridge Exemption From Consideration
children.label: e .—Bridge Exemption From Consideration
children.text: A common post-1945 concrete or steel bridge or culvert (as described in 77 Fed. Reg. 68790) that is exempt from individual review under shall be exempt from consideration under this section.section 306108 of title 54

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Improvements to, or the maintenance, rehabilitation, or operation of, railroad or rail transit lines or elements thereof that are in use or were historically used for the transportation of goods or passengers shall not be considered a use of a historic site under subsection (a), regardless of whether the railroad or rail transit line or element thereof is listed on, or eligible for listing on, the National Register of Historic Places.

children.label: i
children.text: stations; or

children.label: I
children.text: railroad lines that have been abandoned; or

children.label: II
children.text: transit lines that are not in use.

children.heading: .—Clarification with respect to certain bridges and tunnels
children.label: B .—Clarification with respect to certain bridges and tunnels
i
children.text: over which service has been discontinued; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that have been railbanked or otherwise reserved for the transportation of goods or passengers.
